mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion and consumer income growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through increased corporate earnings expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:December 2013 payroll growth of 74,000 persons indicates moderate labor market momentum
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS growth and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll months do not produce significantly higher forward returns than negative payroll months (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate remaining near zero while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains below 2% signals continued monetary accommodation that sustains low equity discount rates and supports equity valuations, producing positive 60-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:January 2014 FEDFUNDS at 0.09% and CPIAUCSL at 233.887 indicate sustained accommodation
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary policy with subdued inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 45% of periods when both conditions are met, or Rank IC of the interaction term falls below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) signals weakening consumer demand and reduced corporate revenue expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as equity prices adjust downward.
evidence_refs: fact:December 2013 RSAFS at 431,879 million dollars shows recent consumer spending levels
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite negative RSAFS signal, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above its 3-month moving average signals expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side strength, supporting corporate profit margins and producing positive 20-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:November 2013 INDPRO at 101.2825 reflects post-recession industrial recovery
regime_hint: economic expansion with rising capacity utilization
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO growth and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive INDPRO periods do not produce significantly higher returns than negative periods (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) both expanding simultaneously signal broad-based demand strength across residential and consumer sectors, producing positive 60-day forward returns through multiplier effects on corporate earnings.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 1,000 thousand units AND RSAFS > 430,000 million dollars
evidence_refs: fact:November 2013 HOUST at 1,091 thousand units and December 2013 RSAFS at 431,879 million dollars indicate synchronized demand recovery
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 40% of periods when both conditions are met, or the interaction term Rank IC falls below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) declining by more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals accelerating labor market tightening and rising wage pressures, which compress equity valuations and produce negative 20-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:December 2013 UNRATE at 6.7% reflects ongoing labor market slack
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerating above 2.5% annualized while federal funds rate remains below 0.25% signals monetary policy lagging economic strength, creating conditions for future rate increases that compress equity valuations and produce negative 60-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Q3 2013 GDPC1 at 15,839.3 billion chained 2017 dollars with FEDFUNDS at 0.09% indicates policy accommodation amid recovery
regime_hint: monetary policy normalization cycle following recovery
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction term Rank IC falls below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining month-over-month while unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises simultaneously signals stagflationary pressures with weakening supply and labor market deterioration, producing negative 5-day forward returns as equity investors reprice risk.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 0.5% month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:November 2013 INDPRO at 101.2825 and December 2013 UNRATE at 6.7% provide baseline for deterioration detection
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
